narrative: USD/JPY firms to 158.93 as the yen tests the cusp of intervention for a sixth straight day; 300bp Fed-BoJ carry sustains the bid despite a strong -1.66 options put skew signaling downside hedging. Reports of a US-Iran nuclear freeze and sanctions waiver drive WTI higher to $103.0, while the IMF warns the BoE must remain flexible on rates. Gold lower -0.21%; equities -0.23%; DXY -0.27%; VIX 18.3. Watch RBNZ rate decision May 27.
